scope: This subject is designed to impart fundamental knowledge on the structure, chemistry and therapeutic value of drugs. The subject emphasizes on structure activity relationships of drugs, importance of physicochemical properties and metabolism of drugs. The syllabus also emphasizes on chemical synthesis of important drugs under each class.
objectives: Understand the chemistry of drugs with respect to their pharmacological activity
Understand the drug metabolic pathways, adverse effect and therapeutic value of drugs
Know the Structural Activity Relationship of different class of drugs
Study the chemical synthesis of selected drugs
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the concept of Antihistaminic agents (H1 and H2 antagonists), Gastric Proton Pump inhibitors and Anticancer agent and evaluate the chemistry to their pharmacological activity, Learn the Structural Activity Relationship (SAR) of the drug
CO2: Discuss about the drugs acting as Anti-anginal agents, Diuretics and Anti-hypertensive agents and identify the synthetic procedures for the preparation of drugs

CO3: Explain the chemistry, mechanism of action, pharmacological importance, and synthetic pathways of drugs used as anti-arrhythmic agents, anti-hyperlipidemic agents, drugs acting on congestive heart failure, and coagulation-modifying agents.

CO4: Compare and contrast the steroidal ring core, the basis of drugs acting on endocrine system and its metabolism. Investigate the concept of Thyroid and understand the drugs used as anti-thyroid agents

CO5: Outline the drugs acting as anti-diabetic agents, interpret the synthesis and understand the mechanism of action and classify the structures of Local anaesthetics and correlate with the biological activity through SAR studies

scope: Course enables the student to understand and appreciate the influence of pharmaceutical additives and various pharmaceutical dosage forms on the performance of the drug product.
objectives: Know the various pharmaceutical dosage forms and their manufacturing techniques.
Know various considerations in development of pharmaceutical dosage forms
Formulate solid, liquid and semisolid dosage forms and evaluate them for their quality
courseOutcomes: CO1: Perform various characterization methods for drug and excipients.
CO2: Perform the manufacturing of tablets and coating of tablets.
CO3: Evaluate the method of preparation of hard and soft gelatin capsules.
CO4: Explain the formulation, manufacturing, and evaluation of parenteral products
CO5: Demonstrate the preparation of various cosmetics, aerosols and classify the packaging materials.

scope: This subject is intended to impart the fundamental knowledge on various aspects (classification, mechanism of action, therapeutic effects, clinical uses, side effects and contraindications) of drugs acting on different systems of body and in addition, emphasis on the basic concepts of bioassay.
objectives: Understand the mechanism of drug action and its relevance in the treatment of different diseases
Demonstrate isolation of different organs/tissues from the laboratory animals by simulated experiments
Demonstrate the various receptor actions using isolated tissue preparation
Appreciate correlation of pharmacology with related medical sciences
courseOutcomes: CO1: Possess a relevant knowledge in basic principles of pharmacology and its recent advances
CO2: Understand the mechanism of drug action and its relevance in the treatment of different diseases.
CO3: Demonstrate isolation of different organs/tissues from the laboratory animals by simulated experiments.
CO4: Demonstrate the various receptor actions using isolated tissue preparation.
CO5: Appreciate correlation of pharmacology with related medical sciences.

scope: The main purpose of subject is to impart the students the knowledge of how the secondary metabolites are produced in the crude drugs, how to isolate and identify and produce them industrially...
objectives: to know the modern extraction techniques, characterization and identification of the herbal drugs and phytoconstituents
to understand the preparation and development of herbal formulation.
to understand the herbal drug interactions
to carryout isolation and identification of phytoconstituents
courseOutcomes: CO1: Brief the basic metabolic pathways leading to the formation of secondary metabolites and to explain the role of tracer techniques in exploring the biogenetic pathway.

CO2: Identify and appreciate the composition, chemistry, therapeutic and commercial stand of various crude drugs reported with secondary metabolites and to describe the general extraction, isolation and analytical methods of the same.

CO3: Schematize the specific isolation and identification techniques of terpenoids, glycosides, alkaloids and resins and sketch out a suitable conventional and modern analytical techniques for the same.

CO4: Detail the industrial production and estimation techniques of successful lead molecules including diosgenin, digoxin, forskolin and to update the current utilization status of these leads in present pharmaceutical market.

CO5: Elaborate the modern methods of extraction along with its instrumentation and applications and to elaborate the latest techniques in chromatographic and spectroscopic analysis related to natural products.

scope: This course is designed to impart basic knowledge on several important legislations related to the profession of pharmacy in India.
objectives: The Pharmaceutical legislations and their implications in the development and marketing
Various Indian pharmaceutical Acts and Laws
The regulatory authorities and agencies governing the manufacture and sale of pharmaceuticals
The code of ethics during the pharmaceutical practice
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the Pharmaceutical legislations and their implications in the development and marketing of pharmaceuticals.
CO2: Describe the various Indian pharmaceutical Acts and Laws.
CO3: Explain the regulatory authorities and agencies governing the manufacture and sale of pharmaceuticals.
CO4: Apply the code of ethics during the pharmaceutical practice.
CO5: Explain the Medical termination of pregnancy, rights to information acts and intellectual property rights acts.

scope: Laboratory training in preformulation studies, tablet manufacturing, coating, capsule evaluation, and parenteral/ophthalmic preparation under aseptic conditions.
objectives: Perform preformulation studies for drug substances and excipients.
Prepare tablets using suitable formulation and manufacturing methods.
Perform coating of tablets and evaluate coating quality.
Prepare and evaluate ophthalmic preparations such as eye drops.
Evaluate hard gelatin capsules for quality control parameters.
Prepare and formulate parenteral preparations under aseptic conditions.
courseOutcomes: CO1: Study the physical and chemical properties of drugs and excipients, and prepare tablets using suitable formulation methods.
CO2: Perform tablet coating to improve stability, appearance, and patient compliance, and evaluate the coated tablets.
CO3: Prepare eye drops under aseptic conditions and evaluate them for clarity, pH, sterility, and stability.
CO4: Evaluate hard gelatin capsules for weight variation, disintegration, dissolution, and content uniformity.
CO5: Prepare parenteral formulations under sterile conditions and evaluate them for sterility, pH, clarity, and stability.

scope: Practical sessions on animal handling, routes of drug administration, simulated experiments on isolated organs, bioassays, and anti-inflammatory/analgesic evaluations.
objectives: Demonstrate the maintenance of laboratory animals, blood collections techniques.
Know about the routes of administration and evaluate the effect of drugs isolated organ of frogs.
Understand about various receptors actions using isolated tissue preparation.
Understand isolation of different organs from the laboratory animals by simulated experiments.
Know about the screening methods of anti-inflammatory and analgesic activity
courseOutcomes: CO1: Describe the importance of physiological salt solutions and to identify the effect of various drugs on isolated frog heart
CO2: Analyze the diuretic activity of drugs in mice/rats and the dose response relationship on animal simulator software
CO3: Evaluate the PD2 value of drugs using guinea pig ileum and interpret the effect of spasmogens and spasmolytics using rabbit jejunum.
CO4: Evaluate the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of medicines through the use of several pharmacological screening methodologies.
CO5: Describe the importance of physiological salt solutions and to identify the effect of various drugs on isolated frog heart and DRC using animal simulator software.

scope: Practical experiments for morphological, histological, and microscopical evaluations of crude drugs, isolation/detection of active principles, and chromatographic/chemical test analyses.
objectives: Perform the morphological evaluation of crude drugs
Carry out the histological and powder microscopical evaluation of crude drugs
Perform the isolation and detection of active phytoconstituents from crude drug sample
Carry out the chromatographic separation and evaluation of chemical constituents in natural drugs
Analyze the unorganized drugs by chemical tests
courseOutcomes: CO1: Report the morphological characteristics, histology and powder characteristics of organized crude drugs, identify and detect the allied species and adulterants.
CO2: Optimize and compose a suitable isolation procedure for bioactive constituents like Sennoside, Diosgenin, and Atropine from crude drug.
CO3: Explain the basic principles and concepts in chromatography, to separate the phytoconstituents based on chromatography using paper as well as TLC plates.
CO4: Distill volatile oil and confirm its identity by thin layer chromatography
CO5: Analyze the unorganized drug using suitable chemical tests.
